title: turnera ulmifolia var. cuneiformis
intro: Turnera ulmifolia var. cuneiformis, also known as the "Prickly Poppy" or "Turnera", is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its unique, prickly leaves and small, yellow flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of Turnera ulmifolia var. cuneiformis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
